back_translation: In the four hundred and eightieth year after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon's reign over Israel, in the month of Ziv, the second month, he began to build the house of the LORD.
The house that King Solomon built for the LORD was sixty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high.
The portico in front of the main hall of the house was twenty cubits long across the width of the house, and projected ten cubits in front of the house.
He made narrow framed windows for the house.
Against the wall of the house he built a structure of rooms all around—around the walls of the house, around both the main hall and the inner sanctuary—and he made side rooms all around.

The lowest floor was five cubits wide, the middle six cubits, and the third seven cubits, for he made offset ledges around the outside of the house so that nothing would be inserted into the walls of the house.
When the house was built, it was built of finished stone from the quarry, so that no hammer or chisel or any iron tool was heard in the house while it was being built.
The entrance to the lowest floor was on the south side of the house; a winding stairway went up to the middle floor, and from the middle to the third.
So he built the house and finished it, and he roofed the house with beams and planks of cedar.
He built the side rooms along the whole house, each five cubits high, attached to the house with beams of cedar.
Then the word of the LORD came to Solomon, saying:

“Concerning this house you are building: if you walk in my statutes and carry out my ordinances and keep all my commandments by walking in them, then I will establish my word with you, which I spoke to David your father.
And I will dwell among the Israelites, and I will not forsake my people Israel.”
So Solomon built the house and finished it.

He lined the walls of the house on the inside with cedar boards, from the floor of the house to the ceiling beams; he overlaid the inside with wood, and he covered the floor of the house with cypress boards.
He partitioned off twenty cubits at the rear of the house with cedar boards from floor to ceiling, to form within it the inner sanctuary, the Most Holy Place.
And the main hall in front of it was forty cubits long.
The cedar inside the house was carved with gourds and open flowers; everything was cedar, no stone could be seen.
He prepared the inner sanctuary within the house to set there the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and twenty cubits high; he overlaid it with pure gold, and he overlaid the altar of cedar.
Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold, and he drew gold chains across the front of the inner sanctuary, which was overlaid with gold.
He overlaid the whole house with gold until the entire house was finished, and he overlaid with gold the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary.
In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olive wood, each ten cubits high.
One wing of the first cherub was five cubits and the other wing five cubits; from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other was ten cubits.
The second cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same size and shape.
The height of the first cherub was ten cubits, and so was the second.

He set the cherubim inside the innermost room of the house. Their wings were spread out, so that the wing of the first cherub touched one wall and the wing of the second cherub touched the other wall, and in the middle of the room their wingtips touched one another.
He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
He carved all the walls of the house all around with carved figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, in both the inner and outer rooms.
He overlaid the floor of the house with gold, in both the inner and outer rooms.
For the entrance of the inner sanctuary he made doors of olive wood, with five-sided doorposts.
The double doors were of olive wood, and he carved on them cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold, spreading the gold over the cherubim and palm trees.
In the same way he made for the entrance of the main hall doorposts of olive wood, four-sided.
The two doors were of cypress wood; the first door had two folding leaves, and the second door had two folding leaves.
He carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, and overlaid them with gold applied evenly over the carving.
He built the inner courtyard with three rows of dressed stone and one row of trimmed cedar beams.
The foundation of the house of the LORD was laid in the fourth year, in the month of Ziv.
And in the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, the eighth month, the house was finished in all its parts and according to all its specifications. So he built it in seven years.
